name: http-server-skeleton
description: src/server/ split: types.ts (wire envelopes), router.ts (pure pattern match — compilePattern + matchRoute, no I/O), errors.ts (BurrowError→HTTP status mapping), response.ts (json/ndjson constructors), routes.ts (route table — every endpoint mirrors a src/lib/client.ts namespace 1:1), server.ts (Bun.serve fetch loop, dispatch, error rendering). Pure router is the testability seam; integration tests target server.ts. Route table mirrors Client namespaces so HTTP layer can't drift from Library API.

content: BurrowError subclass → HTTP status mapping in src/server/errors.ts renderError(): NotFoundError→404, ValidationError→400, CredentialError→401, ToolchainMismatch→409, AgentNotInstalled→424, AgentRuntimeError/SandboxError/SecretResolutionError→502, plain Error→500. Sister table to CLI exitCodeFor (mx-2362a5) — keep them aligned when adding new error classes.

Router distinguishes 404 vs 405 via pathExists(): if any route in the table matches the pathname for any method, return 405 method_not_allowed; otherwise 404 not_found. Trailing slashes are stripped (except for the bare root '/') so /burrows and /burrows/ resolve identically. Param values are decodeURIComponent'd at match time.

Step 2 (pl-5b40) CRUD handlers live in src/server/handlers.ts; handlerFor(client, method, pattern) returns the bound handler or null. routes.ts buildRoutes(client) walks ROUTE_TABLE and uses handlerFor with a 501 stubHandler fallback so streaming routes and POST /burrows (no Client.burrows.create analogue — burrow up materializes workspace, doctor, sandbox profile) stay 501 by design until step 3 lands. Route ordering stays locked in routes.ts so step 7 contract tests have a single seam.

startServer/buildRoutes accept Client | null. With null, every mirrored route returns 501 (preserves the step-1 routing-only test seam — server.test.ts uses null + custom routes for handler-error coverage). With a real Client, handlerFor binds CRUD routes to client.{burrows,runs,inbox,agents} methods and falls back to 501 for streaming/uncovered routes.

handler tests (src/server/handlers.test.ts) replace built-in agents with mocks: built-in claude-code/sapling/codex installCheck() shells out — slow + nondeterministic. Tests unregister built-ins, register makeMockAgent({spawnPerTurn: true}) shaped to match AgentRuntime, and assert on those ids.

HTTP streaming handlers (GET /burrows/:id/events, /runs/:id/stream, /watch) bridge async generators to Response bodies via asNdjsonStream + bridgeAbort in src/server/handlers.ts. bridgeAbort wires request.signal -> per-stream AbortController whose signal is passed to the source generator; ReadableStream.cancel aborts the same controller so generator finally blocks tear down setInterval/abort listeners regardless of which side closes first. Wire bytes match burrow events --json / burrow watch --json exactly because the handlers reuse eventToEnvelope (src/events/render.ts) and stringify DashboardSnapshot directly — never reshape.

burrow serve auth: AuthProvider seam in src/server/auth.ts (NO_AUTH, bearerAuth(token), resolveAuth({noAuth?, token?, env?})). startServer() takes opts.auth (default NO_AUTH). server.ts checks AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS (only /healthz) before auth.authorize(request) → matchRoute. Bearer compare uses node:crypto timingSafeEqual; 401s include RFC 7235 WWW-Authenticate header. Future multi-user lands by swapping AuthProvider — no handler changes.

burrow serve transport: ServeOptions.transport is a discriminated union ({kind:'unix',path} | {kind:'tcp',hostname,port}). server.ts bindUnix unlinks stale socket inode before Bun.serve({unix:path}) (cleared inode unblocks EADDRINUSE on crash recovery); stop() also unlinks. ServeHandle exposes .transport plus a .url string that is purely human-readable ('unix:///path' or 'http://host:port') — fetch() over unix sockets uses Bun's fetch(url, {unix: path}) form, not the .url field.

BURROW_API_TOKEN never appears in logs: createLogger() in src/logging/logger.ts sets pino redact paths covering authorization headers (top-level + req.headers.* in both casings), 'token', 'BURROW_API_TOKEN', and 'env.BURROW_API_TOKEN', censored as [REDACTED]. Defense-in-depth — server handlers don't log headers today, but redact catches accidental {req}-style logging. logger.test.ts asserts the secret literal never appears in output.

Default unix-socket path for burrow serve is <client.paths.cacheDir>/burrow.sock — derived inside runServeCommand from the open Client (src/cli/commands/serve.ts defaultSocketPath). Tests inject defaultSocketPath to avoid touching the real cacheDir. The CLI shim mkdirs the socket parent dir before bind (Bun.serve({ unix }) does not), so a fresh install where cacheDir doesn't exist yet still binds cleanly.

HTTP-backed Client mirror lives at src/lib/http-client.ts; namespace fields (burrows/runs/inbox/events/agents) and method names match src/lib/client.ts so consumers can swap transports. Every method is Promise-returning even where the in-process Client is sync, because network IO can't be sync.

HttpClient rehydrates Date fields (createdAt/updatedAt/destroyedAt for Burrow; queuedAt/startedAt/completedAt for Run; createdAt/deliveredAt for Message; ts for RunEvent) so callers can call .getTime() etc. just like with the in-process Client. Tests that compare timestamps must use a re-read row (server roundtrip drops sub-second precision per mx-9c4605), not the in-memory create result.

HttpClient rehydrates the server's {error: {code, message, hint}} envelope back into matching BurrowError subclasses (NotFoundError, ValidationError, etc.) so consumers keep working with 'instanceof NotFoundError'. Codes the server emits but BurrowError doesn't model (unauthorized, not_implemented, ...) surface as HttpClientError with .status/.code/.hint.

HttpClient transport: pass the same Transport shape that startServer's ServeHandle.transport returns. For unix sockets, internally the URL stays http://localhost/<route> with Bun fetch's {unix: path} option; for TCP it's http://hostname:port. Bearer token (HttpClientOptions.token) is rendered into every Authorization header except /healthz which the server keeps auth-exempt.

name: subprocess-integration-test
description: End-to-end CLI tests spawn 'bun src/cli/main.ts serve --json --port 0 --no-auth' as a Bun.Subprocess with BURROW_DATA_DIR/CONFIG_DIR/CACHE_DIR pointed at fresh tmp dirs. The --json startup banner on stdout (terminated by '\n') is the synchronous handshake — read the first line with stdout.getReader() then drain stdout+stderr in background tasks to avoid backpressure deadlocks. Pre-seed via a parallel Client opened against the same DB (WAL + idempotent drizzle migrations make multi-process access safe). SIGTERM/SIGINT exit cleanly with code 0 in <1s because makeAbortController in cli/main.ts wires signals to handle.stop().

content: src/index.ts re-exports HttpClient + namespace classes + Http* option/filter types from ./lib/http-client.ts, AND Transport/ErrorEnvelope from ./server/types.ts. Consumers construct HttpClient without reaching into ./lib or ./server subpaths. Biome organizeImports sorts re-exports by 'from' path: ./lib/http-client.ts sits between ./lib/client.ts and ./logging/logger.ts; ./server/types.ts sorts between ./secrets/store.ts and ./ship/index.ts.

Streaming endpoints use a single shared 'true'/'false'/'1'/'0' grammar for their boolean toggles via parseStreamBool(raw, label) in src/server/handlers.ts — used by ?follow= on /events and /runs/:id/stream and by both ?once= and ?follow= (alias) on /watch. Specifying both ?once and ?follow on /watch is a 400 (they are inverses). The strict parseBoolean helper is reserved for non-streaming bodies/params (e.g. ?archive= on DELETE /burrows/:id).

BurrowsClient.up() (src/lib/client.ts) wraps runUpCommand (src/cli/commands/up.ts) so the in-process Library API and POST /burrows share one provisioning path. Tests inject materializer/skipDoctor seams via BurrowsClient.setUpOverrides() — the HTTP handler does not accept those fields on the wire (overrides are server-side only), so handler/HttpClient tests must call setUpOverrides on the underlying in-process Client before fetching. Handler maps body→BurrowUpInput then calls client.burrows.up(input); 201 on success.

POST /runs/:id/cancel is idempotent: a run already in a terminal state (succeeded/failed/cancelled) returns its current row with 200, not a 4xx — and the original errorMessage + emitted run_cancelled event are preserved (the second cancel doesn't re-stamp or re-emit). Implemented in RunsClient.cancel() in src/lib/client.ts via a RUN_TERMINAL_STATES.has(current.state) early-return before finalize/append. Lets warren retry safely without special-casing terminal states.

title: DELETE /runs/:id is record-removal post-completion, not force-kill
rationale: Two options were considered for DELETE /runs/:id (per burrow-6739): hard-stop/force-kill of an in-flight run, or record removal of a terminal run. Picked record removal because force-kill would have looked identical to POST /cancel today (no process supervisor wired into Client.runs in V1), making the two endpoints semantically redundant. Record removal is genuinely orthogonal to cancel: cancel is a state transition (queued/running → cancelled), DELETE is post-completion cleanup that physically removes the row. DELETE on a non-terminal run returns 400 (cancel it first); DELETE cascades to events.run_id rows in the same transaction because the FK would otherwise block the delete. Mirrors cleanly into HttpClient.runs.delete() and is documented in SPEC §27 + the OpenAPI doc.

content: OpenAPI spec.ts requestBody supports an optional 'required' field (default true). Set 'required: false' for endpoints whose body is optional (e.g. POST /runs/:id/cancel accepts a bare POST or {reason}). The renderOperation function in src/server/openapi/spec.ts honours op.requestBody.required ?? true. Pair with readJsonBodyOrEmpty() in handlers.ts which returns null for an empty request body instead of 400-ing.

RunsClient.setOnCreated(cb) (src/lib/client.ts) is the create-time hook the RunDispatcher uses to learn about HTTP-enqueued runs. Single-callback registration — setting again replaces the previous; pass null to clear. Intentionally NOT an event-bus subscription: the bus carries persisted events rows and a queued-run notification is a control signal, not a tail-able timeline entry. RunsClient.create calls the hook AFTER the DB insert, before returning to the HTTP handler.

description: Bun.serve's default idleTimeout (10s) force-closes long-lived NDJSON streams (/runs/:id/stream, /burrows/:id/events, /watch) after 10s of silence between events; clients see ECONNRESET and warren's bridgeRunStream dies. Pass idleTimeout: 0 to Bun.serve in src/server/server.ts bindTcp/bindUnix so streaming surfaces are exempt.
resolution: Set idleTimeout: 0 in both bindTcp and bindUnix Bun.serve calls. bun-types omits idleTimeout from UnixServeOptions (the XOR forbids it on the unix branch), so the unix call needs a @ts-expect-error — the runtime honors it identically per Bun docs.
